Preparing for your inspection visit

Getting Ready for Your Appraisal Inspection

A few simple things ahead of time make the visit quick, thorough, and stress-free.

Thanks for having me out to appraise your home. None of this is required — but a little preparation helps me see everything clearly, get in and out efficiently, and give your property its fair and complete evaluation. Here's what helps most.

Access & Logistics

Getting me in and around the property

Unlock everything

Please unlock gates, sheds, outbuildings, and garages so I can access the full property.

Clear the way to utilities

Make sure I can reach the electrical panel, water heater, and furnace or HVAC.

A spot to park

If there's no street parking or parking is tight, freeing up a spot is much appreciated.

Settle your pets

I love animals — but securing or crating them keeps everyone comfortable and helps me not miss anything.

A Couple of Things to Tell Me

Quick answers, no paperwork needed

Permit history

Let me know if any additions or converted spaces — a garage conversion, an added bedroom, an ADU — were permitted. Just what you know is plenty.

Solar details

Tell me whether any solar system is owned, leased, or on a power purchase agreement (PPA).

Differences from public record

If the living area, bedroom count, or bathroom count differs from public record, let me know — and if you know why (a permitted addition, a converted space, a records error), tell me that too.

Helping Me See the Home

If I can't see it, I can't credit it

Turn on the lights

Turning lights on ahead of time is a big help. Switches can be hard to find — or what looks like a switch runs the garbage disposal — and some lights are controlled at the lamp or by a remote I can't find in the dark, or tucked behind a door or towel.

Open the blinds

Open blinds and shades so each room's features are easy to see.

Open up every room

Please make sure every bedroom, bathroom, and living space is accessible and easy to enter.

Point Out the Hidden Improvements

The value that doesn't show on a walkthrough

Show me the invisible upgrades

Point out valuable work I might not see: a new roof, re-pipe, updated electrical panel, foundation repair, new HVAC, added insulation, or upgraded windows.

What to Expect During My Visit

So there are no surprises

Exterior first, then inside

I usually start outside, walking the perimeter of the home, then return to the front door to come in.

A quick visit, lots of photos

I take a lot of pictures, so I'm not there very long — I fill in my notes afterward.

Shoes off indoors

I'll remove my shoes before entering your home.

A Note on Space

Relax — and give me a little room

No need to tidy up

There's no need to clean or declutter. It doesn't affect the appraisal.

Leave me a little room to work

I'll be taking wide-angle photos in every direction. A bit of space helps — standing close by can block the very things I'm trying to capture.

Thank you — I'm looking forward to seeing your home.
